title: Fans Speculate GTA 6 Trailer 3 Could Drop on August 7
summary: Grand Theft Auto community members believe the third trailer for the game may be revealed on August 7, coinciding with Take-Two's fiscal Q1 2027 earnings call. This speculation is based on patterns from previous Rockstar releases where trailers dropped two weeks before investor calls. Some fans also suggest there could be separate cinematic and gameplay trailers.

title: Sony's Physical Disc Ban Announcement Generates More Views Than GTA 6 Trailer
summary: Sony's announcement ending physical disc production for PlayStation games in 2028 has generated approximately 140 million views, surpassing the first GTA 6 trailer's 126 million views. The controversy has overshadowed GTA 6 pre-order news and placed Rockstar in an awkward position as its code-in-box physical edition exemplifies Sony's future digital-only strategy.

title: Sony Remained Silent While GTA 6 Physical Edition Backlash Intensified
summary: Sony has stayed quiet for over a week following massive backlash to its announcement ending PlayStation disc production, leaving Rockstar to absorb criticism for GTA 6's code-in-box physical release. The controversy has spawned petitions and a 'No Disc, No Buy' movement, though early pre-order data suggests demand for the game remains strong despite the outrage.

title: Sony Reportedly Blindsided Publishers and Partners on Disc Ban Decision
summary: A new report reveals Sony did not inform publishers, business partners, or even its own regional offices before announcing the end of physical disc production for PlayStation games in 2028. Major publishers learned about the policy change when Sony announced it publicly, with one executive noting the decision could put jobs at risk and expressing concern about the impact on retail partnerships.

title: Rockstar Developers Threaten Strike Action During GTA 6 Final Crunch
summary: Developers at Rockstar's UK studios are moving forward with plans to unionize ahead of GTA 6's November release, seeking protections on crunch overtime, pay transparency, and flexible working. The unionization effort affects employees at facilities in Edinburgh, Dundee, Lincoln, Leeds, and London, with organizers noting a potential strike during the critical pre-launch period could delay the game or result in a buggy release.
